evals.prompt: $implement-plan add CSV export in a shared production web app that has a runnable Vitest suite
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 auto-selects unit tests and code review without preference questions.
expectations: Records Unit tests: selected, Unit tests source: auto-assessment, and a reason citing executable code plus runnable Vitest.
Records Code review: selected, Code review source: auto-assessment, and a reason citing shared production code.
Records Depth: TDD and uses qa-engineer test-first flow after approval.
Asks neither quality preference question.

evals.prompt: $implement-plan update wording in a documentation-only project
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 auto-skips unit tests and code review without preference questions.
expectations: Records both decisions as skipped with source auto-assessment and evidence-based reasons.
Records Depth: simplify.
Does not assign qa-engineer or invoke code-review-lite.
Still records applicable validation/build/test verification.

evals.prompt: $implement-plan change shared production service code in a project with no runnable unit-test harness
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 skips new unit tests but selects code review without asking.
expectations: Records Unit tests: skipped because no established runnable harness exists.
Records Code review: selected because shared production code changes.
Uses simplify depth and code-review-lite after mandatory build/existing-test verification.
Does not create a test framework automatically.

evals.prompt: $implement-plan target module has conflicting test rules but clearly changes shared production code
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 asks only unresolved unit-test choice.
expectations: Asks unit-test multiple-choice question because evidence conflicts.
Does not ask code-review question because project evidence selects it.
User answer becomes Unit tests source: user.
Plan writing waits until unresolved unit-test choice is answered.

evals.prompt: $implement-plan add service behavior; do not write unit tests and do not run code review
evals.expected_output: Explicit choices override automatic project assessment.
expectations: Records both decisions as skipped with source user and reasons citing explicit instructions.
Asks neither quality question.
Creates no new tests and runs no review flow.
Still runs project build and existing test suite.

evals.prompt: $implement-plan .plans/existing.md with Unit tests: requested and Code review: not requested
evals.expected_output: Complete legacy flags map and resume without duplicate plan or questions.
expectations: Keeps existing plan path.
Maps requested to selected and not requested to skipped with source user.
Does not ask either preference question.
Normalizes legacy fields when main agent next writes plan.

non_goals: Do not infer quality needs from entire multi-project workspace instead of nearest target module.
Do not override explicit user quality choices.
Do not create a test framework automatically when auto-assessment skips tests for missing harness.
Do not weaken mandatory build and existing-suite verification.
Do not inline full plan contents into subagent prompts or let subagents edit plan status.
